summary: eVTOL/UAM addressable market of $4.67B–$28.6B by 2030 with 35–55% CAGR. Airport shuttle/UAM dominates (42.7% of revenue). Archer estimated 8–15% market share by 2030 (195–350 units) pending United Airlines and USAF commercialization. North America CAGR 36–38% provides tailwind; competitive landscape dominated by Joby (first-mover), with Archer's regulatory advantage offering 6–12 month lead time.

extras.metric: eVTOL Aircraft Market Size USD bn by 2030
extras.note: Range reflects different TAM definitions (aircraft-only vs. inclusive of services/infrastructure). Mid-case assumes steady certification and initial service launch by 2028–2029.

extras.metric: North America eVTOL CAGR % per annum 2024–2030
extras.regional_strength: North America outpaces global rate (36–38% vs. 35–55% global), driven by FAA regulatory leadership, strongest customer base (United, USAF), and capital availability

rows.label: Urban Air Mobility (Airport Shuttle + Air Taxi) as % of eVTOL Revenue
rows.value: 42.7% of total eVTOL market revenue in 2025

extras.metric: UAM Revenue as Percentage of Total eVTOL Market %

use_case_breakdown.airport_shuttle: Largest sub-segment; Archer primary target via United Airlines partnership

extras.note: Only UAM percentage quantified at 42.7%; other breakdowns are estimated/inferential

Aviation Week doubled forecast from prior 1,000 unit projection. Implies ~6 major OEMs each delivering 250–400 units by 2030. Consistent with $23.4B TAM ÷ $5M ASP ≈ 4,680 aircraft, suggesting conservative unit delivery through 2030 with acceleration post-2030.

rows.label: Archer Aviation Estimated Market Share 2030
rows.value: 8–15% of global eVTOL TAM (195–350 aircraft units by 2030)

extras.metric: Archer Market Share % by 2030 and Unit Forecast
extras.derivation: Assumption based on: (1) United Airlines order 200 aircraft, (2) USAF pilot program 25–50 aircraft, (3) regional/secondary customers 50–100 aircraft. Total 275–350 units assumed as base/bull case. Bear case 60–100 units if partners execute slowly.
extras.base_case: 220 units (10% share); United ramps to 120 units, USAF 25, regional 75
extras.bear_case: 80 units (3–5% share); delays push revenue to 2028–2029
contingencies: United Airlines operational readiness delay → share compresses 3–5%
Competitor (Joby) captures early market → Archer share capped at 8%
USAF pilot program accelerates → Archer share expands to 15%+
Certification delays beyond 2028 → share falls to 3–5%

rows.label: United Airlines Archer Aircraft Revenue Opportunity (2027–2030)

extras.metric: Archer Aircraft Revenue from United Airlines USD bn
extras.note: United commitment is for 'up to 200' aircraft; actual ramp depends on airport shuttle operational readiness. Represents 10% of global 2,000-unit 2030 forecast. Revenue timing critical: H2 2026 announcement → Q2 2027 first deliveries (bull) vs. 2028 (base) vs. 2029 (bear). Per A2.3, revenue recognition is 'next TSR inflection point.'
extras.reference: A2_3.md (Forward Drivers, Driver #1: Commercial Revenue Launch)

rows.label: USAF/Defense eVTOL Market TAM Estimate (ASSUMED)
rows.value: $50–200 million annually by 2028; $300–500 million by 2030 (upside scenario)

extras.metric: USAF eVTOL Defense TAM USD M per annum 2028–2030
extras.derivation: U.S. Air Force 2027 budget allocates $30.6B for aircraft procurement, but specific eVTOL allocation not publicly detailed. Archer USAF partnership positioned for pilot programs (2026–2027) → potential formal procurement (2028–2030). Conservative estimate assumes $50–200M annually (0.2–0.7% of USAF aircraft budget). Upside ($300–500M) assumes rapid military adoption post-pilot validation.
extras.note: Defense TAM is high-sensitivity for Archer due to USAF partnership exclusivity and government customer validation. However, defense procurement timelines are uncertain and typically multi-year with staged milestones.
